author: Austin Crawford
title: Spring Street Gallery 40th Anniversary
summary: The auction will be including a myriad of items - from prints, originals, paintings, photos, weavings and textiles, to ceramics - you are bound to find a piece you love from your favorite Block Island artists. Bid on your favorite pieces as you sip on wine, enjoy live music and snack on hors-d’oeuvres.
content:
The auction will be including a myriad of items - from prints, originals, paintings, photos, weavings and textiles, to ceramics - you are bound to find a piece you love from your favorite Block Island artists. Bid on your favorite pieces as you sip on wine, enjoy live music and snack on hors-d’oeuvres.

All proceeds of the auction will benefit the Historic Spring Street Gallery to further its non-profit mission of cultivating a creative community on Block Island.

Bidding will start at 5-6:45pm on Saturday, October 8th, 2022 at the Spring Street Gallery.

Join us at Spring Street Gallery to celebrate 40 years of appreciating art, locals and Block Island!

HOPE TO SEE YOU THERE!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Wine Pairing & Sun Farm Oyster Shucking
summary: Join Block Island’s Sun Farm Oysters for Wine & Oysters at Yellow Kitten’s Bar September 7, 5 to 6pm. We are limiting this event to up to twelve people.
content:
Join Block Island’s Sun Farm Oysters for Wine & Oysters at Yellow Kitten’s Bar September 7, 5 to 6pm. We are limiting this event to up to twelve people.

 

Wine expert Sadie Flateman will lead a discussion on various various wines and how they enhance the enjoyment of oysters at Yellow Kittens Bar. Chris Warfel will demonstrate how to shuck oysters, and lead a short discussion on aquaculture on Block Island, and Sun Farm Oysters’ operation. You will enjoy 10 oysters, and generous servings of three pre-selected wines that are more sophisticated  than we previously served (they watch a lot of PBS). Flight of wines presented by wine-professional-cum-oyster-farmer, Sadie Flateman

Please email us at sales@sunfarmoystersbi.com if you have any questions.

There are 6 spaces still available for this event.

Click on the link below to sign up.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Spring Street Gallery 40/40 Art Auction Fundraiser
summary: The Spring Street Gallery is hosting another creative fundraiser this year to celebrate our 40th anniversary.
content:
The Spring Street Gallery is hosting another creative fundraiser this year to celebrate our 40th anniversary.

We are holding a silent art auction including a myriad of items- prints, originals, paintings, photos, weavings and textiles, ceramics- and we would like you to join us! Take home a piece of art from your favorite BI artist.

The Manissees Hotel and Block Island Oyster Bar have been generous enough to offer hors-d’oeuvres, Perry Phillips will provide his local buck a shuck oysters, Cameron Greenlee will be performing live music and of course, our lovely wine and cheese spread that you may be familiar with from our weekly art openings.

Tickets will be $40. While at the auction, if you find a piece (or pieces) you would like to take home with you, your $40 will be deducted from the total of your purchase.

Active bidding on all artwork will be from 5-6:45pm.

All proceeds will benefit the Historic Spring Street Gallery to further its non-profit mission of cultivating a creative community on Block Island.


Mark your calendars to join us on Sunday, September 11 from 5-7!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Mary D. Ball
summary: The "Event of the Season" on Block Island.
content:
The "Event of the Season" on Block Island.
The evening begins with live music and dinner and ends with the crowd dancing into the night to the DJ.

This fundraiser supports the Mary D. Fund. The Mary D. Fund is named for Mary Donnelly - it uses the funds to assist families and individuals with bills they cannot manage in the winter months. Many senior citizens rely on the assistance with heating bills, medical bills and electric bills. 

Tickets available at the link below. or by mailing a check to:  The Mary D. Fund. PO Box 323, Block Island, RI 02807.  The ball will be different this year without Mary D. but she will be there in spirit. Ticket prices are $100 per person.  A reserved table for 10 is $2,500.
address: Corn Neck Rd New Shoreham Washington County

title: Summer Symphony at the Sullivan
summary: A group of musicians from the Eastern Connecticut Symphony is coming to Block Island! They are performing on Wednesday, August 17th, 2022 in one of the island's most glorious settings, The Sullivan House.
content:
A group of musicians from the Eastern Connecticut Symphony is coming to Block Island! They are performing on Wednesday, August 17th, 2022 in one of the island's most glorious settings, The Sullivan House.

A portion of the evening's performance will be presented to Block Island children to bolster their musical education via piano lessons or individual music instruction.

Join us at 5:00pm for mocktails, cocktails and hors d' oeuvres. Performance starts at 6:00 pm. Cash bar available.

Ticks can be purchased at https://www.eventbrite.com/e/summer-symphony-tickets-277009552027
address: Corn Neck Rd New Shoreham Washington County

author: Robert Lucier
title: Chamber of Commerce - Member Meeting @Champlin's
summary: Please join us for our end-of-year member meeting and to celebrate the 2021 season. It will be held this Wednesday, Sept. 29 at Champlin’s Marina and Resort beginning at 5 p.m.
content:
Please join us for our end-of-year member meeting and to celebrate the 2021 season. It will be held this Wednesday, Sept. 29 at Champlin’s Marina and Resort beginning at 5 p.m.

We’re calling this “Positive Growth Through Dialogue.”

Hosted on Champlin’s waterfront event lawn under the Block Island Gourmet three-pole tent, guests will enjoy complimentary tastings of their world-famous mudslides, Landshark beers, Mt. Gay Rum cocktails, and an array of tasty bites including bacon-wrapped scallops, coconut shrimp, peanut chicken satay, and more. A cash bar will also be available for other beers and cocktails. 

Agenda

1. Opening remarks and welcome from the Block Island Chamber
2. Overview of the past season by New Shoreham Police Chief Matt Moynihan
3. Susan Stover will present information on the Block Island Medical Center’s Capital Campaign
4. Neal Murphy of the Old Town Inn will provide background on plans for an island-wide Halloween party
5. William Young of NAMI-BI will provide information on wellness resources for island workers
6. Remarks by Town Manager Maryanne Crawford
7. Open discussion
8. Meet and greet
9. Adjournment
address: 80 West Side Rd - Champlin's Marina - Tent

author: Robert Lucier
title: Mary D. Ball
summary: The Mary D. Ball will take place on August 7th from 5:00 to 9:00 at the Sullivan House. The 27th annual celebration will include dinner and dancing under the tent.
content:
The Mary D. Ball will take place on August 7th from 5:00 to 9:00 at the Sullivan House. The 27th annual celebration will include dinner and dancing under the tent.

Tickets are $100. each OR $2,500 for a reserved table for ten.

There will be no ticket sales at the door this year. We are limiting the number of tickets this year.

Tickets may be purchased by mailing the “Mary D. Ball” a check at PO Box #323 this week.  Later this week tickets will be available on Eventbrite.

Donations may also be mailed to PO Box #323.

We hope to see you there!

title: Summer Symphony at the Sullivan
summary: For the first time ever, a group of musicians from the Eastern Connecticut Symphony is coming to Block Island.
content:
For the first time ever, a group of musicians from the Eastern Connecticut Symphony is coming to Block Island.

They are performing on Tuesday, August 18, 2021 in one of the island’s most glorious settings,

The Sullivan House.  We are delighted that a portion of the evening’s performance will be presented to Block Island children to bolster their musical education via piano lessons or individual music instruction. 

Join us at 5:00 pm for mocktails, cocktails and hors d’ oeuvres.
Performance starts at 6:00 pm
Ticket price is $45.00

Thanks To All Our Sponsors!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Community Tree Lighting at Harbor Church
summary: Come one, come all to the Community Tree Lighting!
content:
Come one, come all to the Community Tree Lighting!
The Block Island Tourism Council invites you to help us light the tree on the Harbor Church lawn.... a tradition for over 16 years!
Wednesday, December 3rd, on the lawn of the Harbor Church. Refreshments will be served!
The fun starts at 5:30 and we will light the tree as close to 6 as possible. (Sometimes we are a few minutes early....don’t miss it!)
We look forward to seeing you there!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Wine & Oysters Great Salt Pond Oyster Co
summary: *Terroir/Merroir; The Geology of Muscadet
content:
*Terroir/Merroir; The Geology of Muscadet 

Partake in the classic pairing of Muscadet wine and oysters!

Geologist Phoebe McMellon will speak on Muscadet’s geologic formation. Sadie Flateman, wine professional turned oyster farmer, owner of Great Salt Pond Oyster Co. will discuss Block Island’s merroir and pairing oysters with wine. Taste how terroir informs wine as merroir does oysters. Demonstrated with a flight of wines from three distinctly different vineyard sites–all from Domaine Luneau Papin (biodynamic)–paired with one dozen GSP oysters.

Thursday, August 10, 2023
Old Island Pub
(2nd floor)
5:30pm – 7:00pm

$75/person
